subject: UX & Research
title: Sunk Cost Fallacy
description: Learn Sunk Cost Fallacy in one minute.

hook.text: Two hours into a terrible movie. You keep watching because you 'already invested' two hours. That's sunk cost.

buildup.text: Sunk cost fallacy: past investment influences future decisions even though it shouldn't. The money is gone.

discovery.text: Arkes and Blumer proved it: people who paid $15 for a ski trip went more than those who paid $5, even in bad weather.

twist.text: Games exploit sunk costs: 'You've spent 200 hours here.' Players keep paying rather than abandoning progress.

climax.text: Progress bars, streaks, and investment tracking all leverage sunk costs. Quitting feels like wasting effort.

punchline.text: Past investment shouldn't drive future choices. But it does. Use it ethically.

quiz.question: How do games exploit the sunk cost fallacy?
options: By showing hours invested and progress, making players feel quitting would waste their effort
By offering refunds for in-game purchases
By limiting playtime to prevent overinvestment
